name: Product Builder CLI End-to-End Workflow
description: Five-phase workflow with conditional branching, parallel steps, task-loop execution, and multi-model review auto-repair.

lite.label: Lite
lite.description: Single CLI, minimal workflow, direct MDX to development without OpenSpec conversion

standard.label: Standard
standard.description: Multi-model review with ccb/cca, manual execution, no automatic loop

full.label: Full
full.description: Complete workflow with automatic loop (Phase 2 uses ralph), multi-model review, and all automation. Openclaw is experimental top-level monitoring.

minimal.description: Minimal directory structure for Lite mode

full.description: Complete directory structure for Standard and Full modes

core_artifacts.description: Core artifacts shared across all modes for upgrade/downgrade compatibility

mode_invariants.description: Enforceable mode-level constraints with unified field structure

degradation_policy.description: Fallback behavior when tools are unavailable

if_ralph_unavailable.behavior: switch to manual execution, preserve all artifacts

if_ccb_cca_unavailable.behavior: skip multi-model review, use single LLM
skip multi-model review, skip OpenSpec generation

observability_profile.description: Logging and metrics depth by mode

ccb.description: Primary LLM CLI for task execution

cca.description: Secondary LLM CLI for multi-model review

ralph.usage: Manages automatic task loop in Phase 2

openclaw.usage: Monitors all tmux terminal windows and unified requirement dispatch
openclaw.note: Not yet tested, will be configured last

phases.name: Requirement Research & Analysis
phases.description: From user requirements to standardized MDX requirement documents

steps.name: Collect User Requirement
steps.description: Extract scope, constraints, and acceptance hints from user input

steps.name: Detect Requirement Type
steps.description: Determine whether requirement is brief (one sentence) or detailed (with planning)

steps.name: Requirement Clarification
steps.description: Ask about target users, core problems, key features, and constraints

steps.name: Requirement Expansion
steps.description: Expand brief requirement into detailed description

steps.name: Competitor Research
steps.description: Use web_search and context7 to find competitors

steps.name: Competitor Analysis
steps.description: Analyze feature comparison, pricing, user reviews, and market positioning

steps.name: Market Positioning
steps.description: Determine differentiation advantages and target market

steps.name: Feature Breakdown
steps.description: Break down core features and sub-features

steps.name: User Story Generation
steps.description: Generate user stories and usage scenarios

steps.name: Technical Feasibility Analysis
steps.description: Evaluate tech stack, dependencies, and risks

steps.name: Generate Requirement Document
steps.description: Integrate all analysis results into structured requirement document

steps.name: Review Requirement Document
steps.description: Multi-model review with auto-repair (up to 3 attempts)

groups.name: Feature & Feasibility Analysis
groups.description: Break down features and evaluate technical feasibility

groups.name: Requirement Document Generation
groups.description: Generate and review standardized requirement MDX

phases.name: Planning
phases.description: From requirement source to reviewed specs and roadmap artifacts.

steps.name: Find Existing Work
steps.description: Discover related jobs, branches, specs, and reusable outputs.

steps.name: Update Capability Tree
steps.description: Rebuild capability map using build-capability-tree.ts.

steps.name: Create Job and Git Branch
steps.description: Create job directory and working branch.

steps.name: Convert Requirements to OpenSpec Format
steps.description: Transform requirement document into OpenSpec format (proposal, tasks, design)

steps.name: Review OpenSpec Documents
steps.description: Run multi-model review gate and auto-repair for OpenSpec quality

steps.name: Parse OpenSpec to Structured Data
steps.description: Extract structured requirement docs from OpenSpec files

steps.name: Check or Infer form.json
steps.description: Use existing form.json when valid, otherwise infer it.

steps.name: Merge to unified.json
steps.description: Merge docs.json and form.json into one execution model.

steps.name: Analyze Capability Dependencies
steps.description: Map feature requirements to capabilities and dependency graph.

steps.name: Generate Development Specs
steps.description: Generate structured specs for development (README + JSON files for ralph automation)

steps.name: Review Development Specs
steps.description: Run multi-model quality gate and auto-repair for generated specs

steps.name: Generate Global Roadmap View
steps.description: Build cross-capability planning graph, milestones, and global todo list

steps.name: Generate Planning Docs
steps.description: Render planning documents from JSON to Markdown (ROADMAP, TODO, DEPENDENCIES)

steps.name: Commit Planning Changes
steps.description: Commit planning artifacts to job branch.

steps.name: Review Planning Results
steps.description: User reviews planning artifacts and decides whether to proceed with execution.

groups.name: Project Context Discovery
groups.description: Discover existing work and analyze system capabilities

groups.name: OpenSpec Generation & Review
groups.description: Generate and validate OpenSpec with multi-model review

groups.name: Implementation Planning
groups.description: Generate development specs, roadmap, and planning documents

phases.name: Execution
phases.description: Execute tasks in a loop with automated testing and repair for each task until all tasks are completed.

steps.name: Start Execution Decision
steps.description: User decides whether to start execution or wait.

steps.name: Create GitHub Issue
steps.description: Create GitHub issue to track this job. Link issue to branch and record issue number.

steps.name: Get Next Task
steps.description: Retrieve next executable task from queue, checking blockedBy dependencies.

steps.name: Execute Task
steps.description: AI Agent implements the current task.

steps.name: Run Automated Tests
steps.description: Run automated tests using testing frameworks (Jest, Vitest, Playwright, etc.) from verification.json.

steps.name: Multi-Model Code Review
steps.description: Review code logic and quality using multiple AI models (gemini, claude, codex).

steps.name: Fix Issue
steps.description: Fix issues found during testing. Add comment to GitHub Issue tracking the fix attempt.

steps.name: Update Specification Document
steps.description: Mark task as completed in specification

steps.name: Update Progress
steps.description: Update progress.json with task completion status.

steps.name: Commit Task
steps.description: Commit task changes to job branch (frontend/backend/OpenSpec).

groups.name: Execution Initialization
groups.description: User decision and GitHub issue creation

groups.name: Task Execution Loop
groups.description: Iterative task execution with automated testing and repair

phases.name: Acceptance
phases.description: Final acceptance with multi-model review and human confirmation before archiving.

steps.name: Final Review
steps.description: Multi-model review gate for final acceptance of all completed work.

steps.name: Aggregate Review Results
steps.description: Aggregate multi-model review results and calculate pass rate.

steps.name: Fix Issues
steps.description: Fix issues found in final review (max 3 attempts). Add comment to GitHub Issue tracking the fix attempt. If exhausted, return to Phase 2.

steps.name: Record Technical Debt
steps.description: Record issues to TECHNICAL_DEBT.md if review passed with minor issues.

steps.name: Human Final Confirmation
steps.description: Final human approval to proceed with archiving and deployment. Only executed after machine review passes.

steps.name: Create Pull Request
steps.description: Create GitHub Pull Request for code review. Link to issue and include review results.

groups.name: Automated Quality Gate
groups.description: Multi-model review with automated repair attempts

groups.name: Pull Request Creation
groups.description: Create GitHub Pull Request for code review

phases.name: Archive
phases.description: Archive OpenSpec, update documentation, and rebuild capability tree.

steps.name: Merge Git Branch
steps.description: Merge feature branch to main (frontend + backend).

steps.name: Archive Specification Document
steps.description: Archive specification document to repository

steps.name: Commit Archive
steps.description: Commit archived OpenSpec to openspec repository.

steps.name: Update Specs
steps.description: Apply changes to capability specifications in specs/.

steps.name: Commit Specs
steps.description: Commit updated specs to waoooo-docs repository.

steps.name: Update Development Docs
steps.description: Generate and update capability documentation.

steps.name: Commit Docs
steps.description: Commit updated documentation to waoooo-docs repository.

steps.name: Update Roadmap Docs
steps.description: Update planning documents (JSON → MDX final state).

steps.name: Commit Roadmap
steps.description: Commit updated roadmap documents to waoooo-docs repository.

steps.name: Rebuild Capability Tree
steps.description: Rebuild capability tree using build-capability-tree.ts.

steps.name: Commit Capability Tree
steps.description: Commit updated capability tree to waoooo-web repository.

steps.name: Create Release
steps.description: Create GitHub Release with changelog and version tag.

groups.name: Code Integration & Archival
groups.description: Finalize code changes and secure project history

groups.name: Knowledge Base Synchronization
groups.description: Update all project documentation and external references

groups.name: System Capabilities Update
groups.description: Refresh the system's understanding of available features

groups.name: Release Publication
groups.description: Package and publish the final release artifacts
